observations.summary: The offer of a peaceful, whole life forgotten by the 'terrible heights' in the source paragraph echoes the motif of ascent and the harsh demands of the heights described in the narrative context, symbolizing a contrast between a desired peaceful existence and the cruel challenges of spiritual or physical elevation.

source_evidence.quote: "I offer you a raft, so that the friction of death shall lose its sting. Follow me. I shall restore your mother's voice to you in the night—dreams in which she speaks comfort unceasing. I shall give you a wife whose hands need not be reddened by lye. Sons who will never know the stench of their grandfather's shame. The sluggish, sweet warmth of a life forgotten by the cruelties of the heights. The life your mother desired for you—peaceful, whole, and forgotten by the terrible heights that demand all."

observations.target_hint: The narrative context's repeated references to 'heights' as a metaphor for spiritual or existential challenges and the motif of 'forgotten' lives and places.
